title: lavatera ligusticum
intro: Lavatera ligusticum, also known as tree mallow, is a perennial plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its large, white or pink flowers and succulent leaves. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lavatera ligusticum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
